source: Bank Negara Malaysia (2024 Malaysia medical cost inflation = 15%; 2023 ~12.6%; 2013-2018 avg ~9-10%/yr); Aon Global Medical Trend Rates Report (2025 Malaysia projected ~12.6%; global ~10%, APAC ~11%); Mercer Marsh Benefits Health Trends 2026 (APAC 2026 projected ~14%); DOSM Consumer Price Index (Malaysia general/headline CPI ~1.8% 2024, ~2% recent).
notes: TWO kinds of figure in this file, with DIFFERENT provenance — do not conflate. (1) rate_series: published Malaysia medical-cost-inflation and general-CPI rates by year from the cited industry surveys/regulators — status 'verified' per row (these are published, each with a source). (2) compounding_model: a DERIVED, ILLUSTRATIVE projection, NOT a measured or published figure and NOT a sourced bill. It applies a CHOSEN illustrative rate of 12%/yr to an illustrative RM10,000 unit of medical treatment over 20 years, vs the same unit under 2%/yr general inflation (DOSM CPI comparator). The 12%/yr is a modelling choice, NOT a computed average: it simply sits within the ~9-15% range Malaysia has recorded (2023 ~12.6%; 2024 actual was higher, at 15% — BNM). Every figure the model emits (the RM96,500 endpoint, the 12% rate, the RM10,000 base) is status 'derived'/'illustrative' — none of them carry verified provenance. The RM10,000 is an illustrative unit to make the compounding gap legible — it is NOT a sourced specific bill or quote. Medical inflation has run ~6-8x general CPI; at 12% vs 2% over 20y the ratio is ~6.5x. Figures rounded sensibly for display. Illustrative model output only; not personalised advice; not guaranteed or assured. Actual costs, inflation, and timelines vary.

medical_cost_inflation_pct.note: actual; higher than the 12% illustrative model rate

medical_cost_inflation_pct.note: projection as published by Mercer Marsh Benefits

medical_to_general_multiple.note: Malaysia medical inflation runs roughly 6-8x general CPI

compounding_model.description: Illustrative compounding of one RM10,000 unit of medical treatment over 20 years. Medical line: RM10,000 x (1.12)^t. General-inflation comparator: RM10,000 x (1.02)^t. Every output here is a modelling choice, not a published or measured figure.
compounding_model.rate_rationale: 12%/yr is a CHOSEN illustrative rate, NOT a computed average. It sits within Malaysia's recorded ~9-15% range (2023 ~12.6%; 2024 BNM actual was higher, at 15%). 2%/yr is the DOSM headline-CPI comparator. Do not present 12% as 'the average' — it is a modelling choice the chart makes legible.
compounding_model.exact_values_note: Unrounded: t=20 medical = RM96,463; general = RM14,859. Displayed values rounded for legibility. Derived/illustrative — not a sourced figure.
